entries.name: Github #4582: double bonds and ring closures
entries.notes: Molblock regression checks doRandom=True outputs for a double-bond/ring-closure case by round-tripping 100 seeded random outputs to the same canonical SMILES. This is in-scope writer behavior but is not yet pinned as a Grimace fixture.

entries.name: basic test
entries.notes: Molblock-backed double-bond/ring-closure writer output is not yet represented by a passing Grimace fixture. Local RDKit 2026.03.1 probing of the small C/N=C/C1=C/CCCCCC1 surface found a one-output exact-support mismatch in directional ring-closure spelling, so this needs directional ring-closure parity work before pinning.
entries.parent: Github #4582: double bonds and ring closures

entries.name: bulk random output order
entries.notes: Bulk random-output section should become a pinned molblock-backed sampled-output or membership fixture; current fixtures do not cover this CHEMBL409450 ring-closure/double-bond regression.
entries.parent: Github #4582: double bonds and ring closures

entries.name: Github #4582 continued: double bonds and ring closures
entries.notes: Aggregate continued #4582 test contains several SMILES/molblock double-bond ring-closure writer regressions still needing fixtures.

entries.name: basics
entries.notes: Small molblock double-bond/ring-closure writer output is not yet represented by a passing Grimace fixture. Local RDKit 2026.03.1 probing found the expected canonical output in support but a one-output exact-support mismatch between C1(=C\CCCCCC1)/C=N/C and C1(=C/CCCCCC1)\C=N\C.
entries.parent: Github #4582 continued: double bonds and ring closures

entries.name: github #3967 part 1
entries.notes: GitHub #3967 part 1 directional ring-closure SMILES writer output is not yet represented by a Grimace fixture. Local RDKit 2026.03.1 probing found exact-support mismatches, so this needs directional ring-closure parity work before pinning as a passing fixture.
entries.parent: Github #4582 continued: double bonds and ring closures

entries.name: github #3967 part 2
entries.notes: GitHub #3967 part 2 directional ring-closure SMILES writer output is not yet represented by a Grimace fixture. Local probing currently hits a stereo carrier-token conflict in Grimace, so this needs runtime parity work before pinning as a passing fixture.
entries.parent: Github #4582 continued: double bonds and ring closures

entries.name: CHEMBL3623347
entries.notes: Large CHEMBL3623347 molblock-backed directional ring-closure writer output is not yet represented by a Grimace fixture.
entries.parent: Github #4582 continued: double bonds and ring closures

entries.name: testBondSetStereo
entries.notes: Manual Bond.SetStereo cis/trans FC=CF writer surfaces are represented by exact serializer fixtures for the resulting RDKit directional-stereo molecules.

entries.name: testBondSetStereoDifficultCase
entries.notes: Manual multi-double-bond stereo isomers are not yet represented by Grimace fixtures. Local RDKit 2026.03.1 probing found 80 sampled outputs per generated isomer versus 32 current Grimace outputs, so this needs coupled directional-stereo parity work before pinning.

entries.name: testBondSetStereoReallyDifficultCase
entries.notes: Legacy stereo-perception coupled double-bond case is in-scope as directional-stereo serialization evidence, but no pinned Grimace fixture currently captures it. Related manual stereo probes expose coupled directional-stereo parity gaps that should be fixed before pinning.

entries.name: testBondSetStereoAtoms
entries.notes: Manually sets double-bond stereo atoms/states and checks isomeric MolToSmiles surfaces. Local RDKit 2026.03.1 probing found that four of the six generated isomer surfaces match Grimace exactly, while the two remaining stable round-tripped directional surfaces produce RDKit random outputs outside current Grimace support; this needs coupled directional-stereo parity work before pinning as a complete passing fixture.

grimace_links.note: Exact all-roots fixtures pin the four complex chiral writer surfaces from Java testIssue157.

entries.name: testIssue157
entries.notes: All Java testIssue157 complex chiral round-trip examples are represented by exact all-roots serializer fixtures.

grimace_links.note: Covers the Java C/C=C/O and C(\C)=C/O normalization pair.

entries.name: testIssue159
entries.notes: Both Java issue159 equivalent E/Z input forms are represented as exact serializer fixtures.

grimace_links.note: Covers the Java testIssue184 molecule in nonisomeric exact-support form.

entries.name: testIssue184
entries.notes: The Java dioxime ring regression is represented by a sampled exact-support fixture for the same input molecule.

grimace_links.note: Exact all-roots fixture pins RDKit 2026.03.1 rooted random outputs for C(/C)=N/O.

entries.name: testIssue185
entries.notes: Rooted branched imine E/Z case is represented by an exact serializer fixture after normalizing RDKit directional-token family inference for selected double-bond carriers.

grimace_links.note: Exact all-roots fixture pins the Java testIssue191 triazine/hydrazone writer surface.

entries.name: testIssue191
entries.notes: Triazine/hydrazone E/Z round-trip is represented by an exact all-roots serializer fixture.

grimace_links.note: Covers the Java testIssue266 kekulized benzene and biphenyl writer outputs.

entries.name: testIssue266
entries.notes: Java benzene and biphenyl aromatic/kekulized whole-molecule writer expectations are represented by writer-membership fixtures.

grimace_links.note: Covers the Java testRootedAt unrooted/root-1 examples for CN(C)C.
Exact support for nitrogen-rooted tertiary amine branch shape.

entries.name: testRootedAt
entries.notes: Java rootedAt wrapper example is covered by shared Python fixture corpus for the same RDKit writer behavior.

grimace_links.note: Covers C[13C](C)(C)C with isomericSmiles false and true.

entries.name: testIsotopes
entries.notes: Java isotope suppression/preservation expectations are represented as nonisomeric and isomeric exact serializer fixtures.

grimace_links.note: Covers the Java explicit and post-cache-update protonated pyrrolidine forms.

entries.name: testBug1670149
entries.notes: Both protonated ring normalization surfaces from the Java regression are represented by exact serializer fixtures.

grimace_links.note: Covers the Java cyclohexyl halide, benzene, pyridine, and pyrrole normalization surfaces.
Membership fixtures pin the specific Java expected writer outputs for this bug.

entries.name: testBug1719046
entries.notes: Java nonisomeric chirality suppression and aromatic normalization expectations are represented by exact serializer and writer-membership fixtures.

grimace_links.note: Covers the Java F/C=N/Cl and conjugated diene stereo output surfaces.
Membership fixtures pin the exact Java imine expected strings.

entries.name: testBug1842174
entries.notes: Java imine and conjugated diene stereo-orientation expectations are represented by exact serializer and writer-membership fixtures.

entries.name: testBug1844617
entries.notes: Complex fused alkaloid stereochemistry round-trip cases are not yet represented by pinned Grimace fixtures.

entries.name: testRingStereochem
entries.notes: Java test checks RDKit ring stereochemistry warning/property behavior around MolToSmiles calls, not a Grimace-supported writer surface.
